exercises.title: Simulate File Reading with a Multi-line String
exercises.description: Practice the same patterns used when reading files, but using a multi-line string as the 'file content'. Count lines, strip newlines, and find lines containing a keyword.
exercises.starterCode:
# In a real program this would come from open('data.txt').read()
# In Pyodide we simulate it with a string
file_content = """Python is a versatile language.
It is used for web development.
Python supports data science and machine learning.
Many developers love Python for its simplicity.
Python has a large standard library."""

# TODO: Split file_content into a list of lines

# TODO: Print the total number of lines

# TODO: Print each line with its line number (starting from 1)
# Format: "1: Python is a versatile language."

# TODO: Find and print all lines containing 'Python' (case-sensitive)
# Print: "Lines with 'Python': 4"
exercises.expectedOutput:
5
1: Python is a versatile language.
2: It is used for web development.
3: Python supports data science and machine learning.
4: Many developers love Python for its simplicity.
5: Python has a large standard library.
Lines with 'Python': 4
hints: Use enumerate(lines, 1) to get 1-based line numbers
sum(1 for line in lines if 'Python' in line) counts matches

exercises.title: Parse a Config-style String
exercises.description: Read a 'config file' represented as a multi-line string. Skip comment lines (starting with #) and empty lines. Parse KEY=VALUE pairs into a dictionary.
exercises.starterCode:
config_text = """# Application Settings
# Generated on 2024-01-15

APP_NAME=My Python App
VERSION=1.0.0
DEBUG=false

# Database Settings
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_PORT=5432
DB_NAME=myapp_db"""

# TODO: Parse config_text into a dict
# Skip lines starting with '#' and empty lines
# Split each remaining line on '=' to get key and value

config = {}
# TODO: Fill config dict from config_text

# TODO: Print each key and value sorted by key
# Format: "APP_NAME = My Python App"

# TODO: Print total number of settings found
# Format: "Total settings: 6"
exercises.expectedOutput:
APP_NAME = My Python App
DB_HOST = localhost
DB_NAME = myapp_db
DB_PORT = 5432
DEBUG = false
VERSION = 1.0.0
Total settings: 6
hints: key, _, value = line.partition('=') splits on first '='

exercises.title: Word Frequency from Text
exercises.description: Analyze a multi-line text 'file': count word frequency, find the top 5 most common words, and count unique words.
exercises.starterCode:
text = """the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
the dog barked at the fox and the fox ran away
the lazy dog slept all day while the fox played"""

# TODO: Split text into words (split on whitespace)

# TODO: Build a word frequency dict

# TODO: Print the 5 most common words and counts, sorted by count descending
# Format: "the: 8"

# TODO: Print the number of unique words
# Format: "Unique words: 11"
exercises.expectedOutput:
the: 8
fox: 4
dog: 3
lazy: 2
and: 1
Unique words: 11
hints: Use a dict and word_count.get(w, 0) + 1 pattern
sorted(word_count.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:5] for top 5

warmup.title: Quick Recall: String Splitting
warmup.description: What does this code print? These are the same operations used when reading files line by line.
warmup.starterCode:
text = "line one\nline two\nline three"
lines = text.splitlines()
print(len(lines))
for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
    print(f"{i}: {line}")
warmup.expectedOutput:
3
1: line one
2: line two
3: line three
